summary: Canonical repair path remains #93897. Current main at 8ce486a3bef4525128e83b807634a189da62cfcc still deletes plugins.entries.openai-codex after only copying it when plugins.entries.openai is absent, so the dual-key data-loss bug is still real. #93897 is narrow and maintainer-editable, but it is not merge-ready: the hydrated artifact shows unresolved review comments about conflict warnings and accurate change messages, failed Real behavior proof, and failing relevant checks. Plan repair on the contributor branch, preserving @lsr911 credit and #93891 reporter context.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93897 so the legacy plugins.entries.openai-codex -> plugins.entries.openai migration preserves non-conflicting legacy entry data, emits accurate merge/rewrite messages, and reports true conflicts as warnings instead of silently dropping values.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(doctor): preserve legacy openai-codex plugin entry fields
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Repairs #93897 on the contributor branch from @lsr911.
- Preserves non-conflicting `plugins.entries.openai-codex` fields when `plugins.entries.openai` already exists.
- Reports an accurate merge message for the dual-key path and warning messages for true key conflicts instead of silently dropping legacy values.
- Adds regression coverage for the dual-key migration case from #93891.

## Credit
Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 by @lsr911.
Issue and review context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 by @Nas01010101.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- Re-run Real behavior proof for #93897 after the repair lands on the PR branch.
credit_notes: Preserve @lsr911 as the source PR author for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897.
Preserve issue/review attribution for @Nas01010101 from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 and #93897 review comments.
PR release-note context should mention the doctor migration now preserves dual openai/openai-codex plugin entry data and warns on conflicts.
